The "'''Ewok Celebration'''," commonly known as "'''Yub Nub'''," is a [[1983]] song featured in [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']]. It was published by [[Bantha Music]] and is administered by [[Warner-Tamerlane Publishing Corporation]].

The song appears in the final scene of the film during the celebration in the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] village. It was removed from the film in the [[List of changes in Star Wars re-releases#Return of the Jedi|Special Edition]] and replaced with a new piece by [[John Williams]] entitled "[[Victory Celebration]]."

The music was written by John Williams, the [[Ewokese/Legends|Ewokese]] lyrics were written by [[Industrial Light & Magic|ILM]] sound designer [[Ben Burtt]], and the English ([[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]]) lyrics were written by John Williams's son, [[Joseph Williams]].

Also called the '''Ewok Freedom Song''', it was performed by the [[London Symphony Orchestra]]. The Ewokese lyrics were sung by the Oakland Inspirational Choir, who performed other Ewokese sounds for the film.<ref>[[Galactic Phrase Book & Travel Guide (real-life book)|''Galactic Phrase Book & Travel Guide'']]</ref>

==English (Basic) lyrics==
<poem>
Freedom, we got freedom;
And now that we can be free,
Come on and celebrate.

Power, we got power;
And now that we can be free,
It's time to celebrate.

Celebrate the freedom;
Celebrate the power;
Celebrate the glory;
Celebrate the love.

Glory, we found glory.
The power showed us the light,
And now we all live free.

<nowiki>*</nowiki>Celebrate the light; (Freedom!)
Celebrate the might; (Power!)
Celebrate the fight; (Glory!)
Celebrate the love.
Celebrate the love.
Celebrate the love.

Glory, we found glory.
The power showed us the light,
And now we all live free.

<nowiki>*</nowiki>Celebrate the light; (Freedom!)
Celebrate the might; (Power!)
Celebrate the fight; (Glory!)
Celebrate the love.
Celebrate the love.
Celebrate the love.
Celebrate the love.<ref name="MakingOfROTJ">''[[The Making of Return of the Jedi]]''</ref>
</poem>

==Continuity==
The original 1983 CD/LP version of the [[Star Wars Episode VI: Return of the Jedi (soundtrack)|''Return of the Jedi'' soundtrack]] (as well as the 1993 [[Star Wars Trilogy: The Original Soundtrack Anthology|''Anthology'']]) utilizes a more subdued and alternate take of the theme which was different from the film version.
